---
title: "Feature Discrimination Visualization"
subtitle: ""
date: last-modified
sidebar: auto
number-sections: false
toc: true
author:
  - Jumbong Junior

categories: []
tags: [""]
title-block-banner: false
format:
  html:
    mainfont: Times New Roman
    fontsize: 1.1em

jupyter: python3

---


```{python}
import pandas as pd
import numpy as np
from scipy.stats import chi2_contingency

import importlib
import src.data_analysis.feature_discrimination_plots as utils

importlib.reload(utils)
from src.data_analysis.feature_discrimination_plots import plot_continuous_vs_categorical, contingency_analysis, plot_grouped_bar
from config import data_path, data_output_path

```

```{python}
path_datasets = data_path
figures_output_path = data_output_path / "figures"
figures_output_path.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
```

```{python}
train_imputed = pd.read_csv(f"{path_datasets}/train_imputed.csv")
```





```{python}
plot_continuous_vs_categorical(
    df=train_imputed,
    continuous_var="person_income",
    categorical_var="def",
    category_labels={0: "No Default", 1: "Default"},
    figsize=(14, 12),
    sample=5000
)
```

```{python}
results = contingency_analysis(
    df=train_imputed,
    var1="def",
    var2="person_home_ownership",
    normalize=None
)
```

```{python}
# Contingency table.
contingency = pd.crosstab(
    train_imputed["person_home_ownership"],
    train_imputed["def"],
    margins=True
)
print("=== Contingency table (counts) ===")
print(contingency)

# Row-normalized frequencies.
contingency_norm = pd.crosstab(
    train_imputed["person_home_ownership"],
    train_imputed["def"],
    normalize="index"
).round(3) * 100
print("\n=== Default rate by modality (%) ===")
print(contingency_norm)

# Chi-square test.
ct = pd.crosstab(train_imputed["person_home_ownership"], train_imputed["def"])
chi2, p_value, dof, expected = chi2_contingency(ct)

n = ct.values.sum()
cramers_v = np.sqrt(chi2 / (n * (min(ct.shape) - 1)))

print(f"\n=== Chi-square test ===")
print(f"Chi²      : {chi2:.4f}")
print(f"p-value   : {p_value:.4f}")
print(f"Degrees of freedom: {dof}")
print(f"Cramer's V: {cramers_v:.4f}")

if p_value < 0.05:
    print("Statistically significant association (p < 0.05)")
else:
    print("No statistically significant association (p >= 0.05)")

# Cramer's V interpretation.
if cramers_v < 0.10:
    strength = "very weak"
elif cramers_v < 0.20:
    strength = "weak"
elif cramers_v < 0.30:
    strength = "moderate"
else:
    strength = "strong"
print(f"Association strength: {strength} (V = {cramers_v:.3f})")


plot_grouped_bar(
    df=train_imputed,
    cat_var="def",
    subcat_var="person_home_ownership",
    normalize="index",
    title="Default Rate by Home Ownership",
    output_path=figures_output_path / "default_rate_by_home_ownership.png"
)
```


```{python}
print(train_imputed.dtypes)
continuous_vars = [
    "person_income",
    "person_age",
    "person_emp_length",
    "loan_amnt",
    "loan_int_rate",
    "loan_percent_income",
    "cb_person_cred_hist_length"
]

qualitative_vars = [
    "person_home_ownership",
    "cb_person_default_on_file",
    "loan_intent",
    "loan_grade",
]
```
